skill.name: specdd-orient
skill.description: Prepare an AI agent to understand a SpecDD project before later work, without editing files.
skill.summary: Orient an agent in an existing SpecDD repository before follow-up tasks.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography, but the cited lines are markdown paths, skill names, or descriptive text. No prompt injection, malware intent, network access, credential access, or destructive action was found. The only residual concern is a read-only instruction to check git status during orientation.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backticks on lines 14, 16, 22, 23, 24, and 52 as Ruby shell execution. These are markdown inline code markers around SpecDD file paths or a skill name, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain markdown inline code syntax only. No Ruby code, shell interpolation, or command execution syntax is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Not Supported By Context
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 3 as a weak cryptographic algorithm. That line is the skill description and contains no cryptographic operation, library call, hash algorithm, or security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is frontmatter prose describing when to use the skill. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, cipher use, or any cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Read-Only Git Status Check May Invoke an External Command
low_findings.description: The workflow asks the agent to check current git status. This is a legitimate read-only repository inspection step, but it may involve an external git command in some environments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction clearly asks for git status, which commonly maps to an external command. The risk is low because the operation is read-only and tied to repository orientation.

content.value_statement: SpecDD repositories require agents to understand project authority before useful follow-up work. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through read-only orientation steps.
actual_capabilities: Confirms that a repository has a SpecDD bootstrap file before continuing.
Reads the bootstrap chain in the defined project order.
Identifies the root or main project spec named by the bootstrap chain.
Inspects top-level specs and repository shape for orientation context.
Checks git status so later work can separate existing changes from new edits.
Reports a short readiness summary with files read and immediate blockers.
limitations: Does not edit files or implement requested changes.
Does not perform deep analysis unless the user asks for it.
Does not create SpecDD files for a non-SpecDD project.
Does not grant write authority for later tasks.

use_cases.title: Prepare for a SpecDD Task
use_cases.description: Use the skill before asking an agent to change code in an existing SpecDD repository.

use_cases.title: Understand Repository Authority
use_cases.description: Use the skill to learn which bootstrap and spec files govern later project work.

use_cases.title: Start a Read-Only Handoff
use_cases.description: Use the skill when a new agent session needs project context without changing files.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Orientation
prompt_templates.scenario: Start a new session in a known SpecDD project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-orient to get oriented in this SpecDD project. Do not edit files.

prompt_templates.title: Orientation With Focus Area
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare for later work in one part of the repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-orient and focus on the specs most relevant to the API layer. Keep the report short.

prompt_templates.title: Readiness Before Implementation
prompt_templates.scenario: Confirm project authority before asking for code changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-orient before implementation. Report the bootstrap files read, main authority found, and any blockers.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Handoff Preparation
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare a later agent task with clear context boundaries.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-orient for read-only context. Identify the active bootstrap chain, root spec, relevant top-level specs, repository shape, and git status.

output_examples.output: Orientation complete. Read the bootstrap chain and root project spec. Main authority comes from the project bootstrap. No immediate blocker found.

output_examples.input: Orient before working on the documentation area.
output: Orientation complete for the documentation area.
Bootstrap and root spec context were reviewed.
Relevant top-level documentation specs were checked.

output_examples.input: Check whether this is ready for a follow-up coding task.
output_examples.output: The project is ready for a follow-up task. Future edits still need normal SpecDD resolution for the exact target path.

best_practices: Run this skill before a concrete follow-up task in an unfamiliar SpecDD repository.
Keep the prompt focused on orientation and defer implementation requests.
Ask for exact files read when you need a clear handoff record.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to edit, refactor, or review code directly.
Do not use orientation results as permanent authority for future tasks.
Do not run it in a repository that lacks a SpecDD bootstrap file.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an agent understand an existing SpecDD project before later work begins.

faq.question: Does it modify files?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly keeps orientation read-only.

faq.question: Can it adopt SpecDD in a new project?
faq.answer: No. It stops if the project lacks the required SpecDD bootstrap file.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why does it check git status?
faq.answer: It records existing repository changes so later edits can avoid confusing old work with new work.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The confirmed behavior is read-only, and the static high-risk findings were false positives.
